evals.prompt: I'm building a B2B SaaS for small accounting firms that want to automate client tax filing. The firm admin creates the account, adds accountants, accountants upload client docs, the system extracts data and pre-fills tax forms, then the accountant reviews and submits to the tax authority. There are two user types: firm admin and accountant. Map the full customer journey for the accountant persona.
evals.expected_output: A complete to-be journey map with 4-8 phases covering the accountant's experience from login through submission, with happy paths, exceptions, touchpoints, and a valid Mermaid diagram. Should focus on the accountant persona only, not the admin.

evals.prompt: We have an existing e-commerce app and we're adding a new 'wishlists' feature. Users can create multiple wishlists, add products, share wishlists with friends via link, and friends can mark items as 'bought' so there are no duplicate gifts. I only want the journey for the wishlist sharing and gift-coordination part, not the full e-commerce experience.
evals.expected_output: A narrowly scoped to-be journey map covering only the wishlist sharing and gift coordination flow (not browsing/purchasing). Should have 3-5 phases, respect the narrow scope, and include the multi-user interaction (sharer + friend).

evals.prompt: make me a journey map for my app
evals.expected_output: The skill should recognize this as too vague and ask clarifying questions about what the app does, who the users are, and what scope to cover. Should NOT generate a full journey map from this input alone.
